title: Cap length-prefixed binary frames safely
prompt: Harden `LengthPrefixedDecoder::read` so an untrusted 64-bit payload length is rejected before it can overflow size calculations or trigger a reservation beyond the decoder's configured maximum. A length greater than `max_payload_size()`, or one for which the 8-byte prefix plus payload cannot be represented by `size_t`, must return `DecodeStatus::kFrameTooLarge` without allocating. A rejected result has an empty payload and reports zero for both `bytes_consumed` and `bytes_needed`. Preserve the existing contract for incomplete prefixes and payloads, exact-limit frames, trailing input, and zero-copy payload views for complete valid frames. Do not modify the tests. Verification is offline and succeeds with `bash tests/run_tests.sh` from the repository root.
